Event: Gimli demands to know what's happening beyond the wall at Helm's Deep
Explanation: Seeking information in battle slightly aids his awareness with no harm to others.

Event: Legolas mockingly asks Gimli if he wants a description or a box to stand on
Explanation: Legolas amuses himself at Gimli’s expense with mild teasing.

Event: Gimli glares at Legolas for the first suggestion
Explanation: Glaring vents irritation but gains nothing and slightly dampens camaraderie.

Event: Gimli laughs at the "box to stand on" joke
Explanation: Laughter improves his own mood and reinforces friendly banter.

Event: Gimli wields ancestral axes passed down from his forefathers
Explanation: Using inherited high-quality weapons benefits him and honors family legacy.

Event: Gimli displays affection through gruff insults toward the Fellowship
Explanation: His abrasive endearments maintain bonds without real harm.

Event: Gimli shoves through a crowd roaring threats while searching for presumed-dead Aragorn
Explanation: Forces his way to Aragorn’s side, jostling others to satisfy his urgent concern.

Event: Gimli hugs Aragorn and blesses him after the faux "I'm gonna kill him" outburst
Explanation: Open affection strengthens their relationship and morale.

Event: Gimli scolds Merry and Pippin for feasting and smoking after the long search
Explanation: Light reprimand expresses care and reinforces group norms.

Event: Gimli fights back-to-back with Aragorn at Helm's Deep
Explanation: Coordinated fighting boosts both his survival and the defense effort.

Event: Gimli eagerly enters the thick of fighting as a Blood Knight
Explanation: His enthusiasm for battle increases his own glory and helps his side, despite risk.

Event: Gimli competes with Legolas in a kill-count contest on battlefields
Explanation: Rivalry motivates both to fight harder, benefiting themselves and allies.

Event: Gimli taunts a Warg with "Bring yer pretty face to my axe!"
Explanation: Provoking the foe into close combat suits his strengths and aids the fight.

Event: Gimli shouts in Moria, "Let them come! There is one Dwarf yet in Moria who still draws breath!"
Explanation: Bravado steels his own courage and signals readiness to allies.

Event: Gimli struggles to keep up while running across Rohan
Explanation: Exertion strains him without noted benefit beyond just not falling behind.

Event: Gimli spends the Warg fight stuck under a pile of corpses
Explanation: His situation removes him from effective combat with no clear gain.

Event: Gimli fails to see over the wall at Helm's Deep due to short stature
Explanation: Loss of situational awareness hinders him, with no benefit shown.

Event: Gimli wears chainmail that is too big for him
Explanation: Ill-fitting armor hampers movement or comfort without compensating advantage.

Event: Gimli is tossed by Aragorn because he can't make the long jump himself
Explanation: Being thrown puts him into the fight and over the gap, aiding both him and allies.

Event: Gimli drinks so much ale he passes out while Legolas barely feels it
Explanation: Overindulgence harms him and contributes nothing useful to others.

Event: Gimli gets spooked about entering the Paths of the Dead
Explanation: Fear increases his own distress without affecting the outcome.

Event: Gimli almost falls off a staircase into a chasm in Moria
Explanation: A near-fatal misstep threatens him with no compensating benefit.

Event: Legolas grabs Gimli by the beard to save him from falling
Explanation: Quick action preserves Gimli’s life with no downside.

Event: Gimli asks only to look on Galadriel one last time as a gift
Explanation: A modest, respectful request deepens mutual regard without cost.

Event: Gimli delivers snark after Aragorn beheads the Mouth of Sauron
Explanation: Gallows humor lightens tension and boosts group morale.

Event: Gimli, believing the last battle is hopeless, declares "What are we waiting for?" and presses onward
Explanation: He accepts likely death to support a desperate, necessary gambit.

Event: Gimli initially behaves antagonistically toward elves and promotes dwarven superiority
Explanation: Racial posturing satisfies his pride while straining potential alliances.

Event: Gimli becomes friendly with Legolas and admires Galadriel
Explanation: Overcoming prejudice strengthens crucial cross-racial bonds.

Event: Gimli refuses to eat Eowyn's stew despite being unfed for days
Explanation: His pickiness harms his own nourishment and mildly offends the cook.

Event: Gimli maintains a body-count competition as a friendly rivalry with Legolas
Explanation: Mutual competition drives both to greater battlefield effectiveness.

Event: Gimli insists on identifying others mainly by race rather than by name
Explanation: Habit flatters his own group identity while risking social friction.

Event: Gimli shouts Aragorn's name and jumps into a horde to protect the downed man at Helm's Deep
Explanation: Self-endangerment protects an ally and key leader, benefitting them both.

Event: Gimli forms a deep friendship with Aragorn and Legolas, healing the elf–dwarf rift
Explanation: Enduring bonds greatly enrich his life and inter-racial peace.

Event: Gimli deliberately pretends his horse fall was intentional ("It was deliberate")
Explanation: Self-deprecating cover preserves his dignity and amuses others harmlessly.

Event: Gimli becomes the first dwarf to form a true friendship with an elf
Explanation: The friendship profoundly benefits both and sets a historic positive precedent.

Event: Gimli remains steadfastly loyal to the Free Peoples of Middle-earth
Explanation: His unwavering allegiance furthers their cause and gives him purpose and honor.

Event: Gimli leaps into the thick of combat and keeps hacking until surrounded by corpses
Explanation: Relentless aggression produces major battlefield gains despite personal risk.

Event: Gimli eagerly "jumps at the call" to enter battles
Explanation: Readiness to fight supports every major engagement and his own warrior identity.

Event: Gimli resists all temptation from the One Ring
Explanation: Immunity protects his integrity and removes a potential internal threat to allies.

Event: Gimli attempts to destroy the One Ring with a swing of his axe
Explanation: He loses a good axe but quickly tests the most straightforward solution that would have saved all if it had worked.
